channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Venezuela reels from earthquakes as rescuers scramble to find survivors
item.description: As Venezuela begins counting the cost of its deadliest quake disaster in over a century, a shattered economy and struggling health system threaten to slow recovery efforts.
item.content:encoded:
As Venezuela begins counting the cost of its deadliest quake disaster in over a century, a shattered economy and struggling health system threaten to slow recovery efforts.

(Image credit: Pedro Mattey)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: Reflecting Pool liner was cut with a sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
item.description: A top official at the National Park Service says a liner along the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was cut with a sharp knife or razor this month, causing damage to the foam sealant installed as part of a $16 million rehabilitation project.
item.content:encoded:
A top official at the National Park Service says a liner along the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was cut with a sharp knife or razor this month, causing damage to the foam sealant installed as part of a $16 million rehabilitation project.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: UN agency pauses evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz after attack on vessel
item.content:encoded: (Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi/AP Photo/Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after its costly refurbishment
item.description: The decision was announced Thursday during a briefing on royal finances at which Charles became the first British monarch to reveal the taxes he paid to the government.
item.content:encoded:
The decision was announced Thursday during a briefing on royal finances at which Charles became the first British monarch to reveal the taxes he paid to the government.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What made the deadly Venezuelan earthquakes different
item.description: It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.
item.content:encoded:
It appears the two big earthquakes in Venezuela that occurred in rapid succession may have involved two separate fault lines. Several faults intersect in this tectonically complex region.

(Image credit: Edilzon Gamez)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: 'The Bear' is back in the kitchen
item.description: In some ways, the fifth and final season of The Bear feels less daring — but after four seasons, the small wins mean more.
item.content:encoded:
In some ways, the fifth and final season of The Bear feels less daring — but after four seasons, the small wins mean more.

(Image credit: FX)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo dominates the pop charts in her new album's first week
item.description: The first-week numbers for Olivia Rodrigo's third album, you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love, are a massive milestone for the pop star.
item.content:encoded:
The first-week numbers for Olivia Rodrigo's third album, you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love, are a massive milestone for the pop star.

(Image credit: Xavi Torrent/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: Photos: See Venezuela destruction after earthquakes
item.description: The earthquakes were Venezuela's largest in over a century.
item.content:encoded:
The earthquakes were Venezuela's largest in over a century.

(Image credit: Federico Parra)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: The Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border
item.description: By a 6-3 vote, the high court ruled that federal law allows the government to stop asylum seekers from physically setting foot in the United States, effectively keeping them from applying for asylum.
item.content:encoded:
By a 6-3 vote, the high court ruled that federal law allows the government to stop asylum seekers from physically setting foot in the United States, effectively keeping them from applying for asylum. 

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Trump can begin deportations of Syrian, Haitian TPS holders, Supreme Court says
item.description: Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.
item.content:encoded:
Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
